---
title: "My hosts.deny"
date: 2009-05-25
forum: Security
---

### Post by lazyart on 2009-05-25
For anyone interested, I'm posting my hosts.deny file.  It contains IP addresses that have tried dictionary attacks via SSHD or VSFTPD.  In addition, while hunting for the owner of one of the ip address I stumbled across a huge list of other attacking address (yep, that address was in the list).

Use with caution.  I have not verified this by any means.  I'm just quite tired of the attacks.  After adding this list my dictionary attacks dropped to 0 over 24 hours.

Copy and paste into your own /etc/hosts.deny if interested.

```
sshd: 200.30.176.205
```

### Post by bodhi.zazen on 2009-05-26
Nothing like reviewing your ssh logs to instill paranoia :)

I find 99.9 % of these things went away when I changed to a non default port. Easier to change to port 4444 then maintain deny hosts.

Better then both of these - Use keys and a strong password.

### Post by standingchair on 2009-12-03
thank you, i appreciate your hard work - both of you
i figured out someone was using a Brazilian proxy to try and access my box via ssh
changed my port and added your list
thanks again
